articles.title: Heatwave Continues In AP; Over 41°C In 207 Mandals - Deccan Chronicle
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Mandals likely to experience heatwave conditions are located in Vizianagaram, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitarama Raju, Eluru, Kakinada and East Godavari districts: Reports
VISAKHAPATNAM: Heatwave conditions continued across Andhra Pradesh on Friday, with temperatures above 41°C recorded in 207 mandals spread across 22 of the state’s 28 districts.
In its forecast for Saturday, the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority said severe heatwave conditions were likely in 14 mandals, while another 28 mandals could experience heatwave conditions. High temperatures are expected in North Coastal Andhra and Rayalaseema regions.
Mandals likely to face severe heatwave conditions include Hiramandalam and Pathapatnam in Srikakulam district, and Balijipeta, Garugubilli, Gummalakshmipuram, Jiyyammavalasa, Komarada, Kurupam, Pachipenta, Palakonda, Parvathipuram, Salur, Seethampeta and Seethanagaram in Parvathipuram Manyam district.
Mandals likely to experience heatwave conditions are located in Vizianagaram, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitarama Raju, Eluru, Kakinada and East Godavari districts.
Salur in Parvathipuram Manyam district recorded the highest temperature of 45.2°C on Friday, followed by Nandavaram in Kurnool district at 43.7°C, Gospadu in Nandyal district at 43.6°C and Therannapalli in Anantapur district at 43.5°C.

articles.title: Heatwave grips Andhra Pradesh; temperature hits 45°C - News9live
articles.full_text:
Heatwave grips Andhra Pradesh; temperature hits 45°C
Severe heatwave is likely in 14 mandals while heatwaves are likely in 28 mandals on Friday, APSDMA said.
Amaravati: Hot, humid and discomfort conditions are likely in parts of Coastal AP, Yanam and Rayalaseema over the next three days, IMD Amaravati said.
AP State Disaster Management Authority MD Prakhar Jain said that the heat wave will continue in the state. He said that temperatures above 41 degrees were recorded in 207 mandals on Friday. On Saturday, severe heatwave is expected in 14 mandals and another 28 mandals are likely to experience heatwave. He said that maximum temperatures are likely to be recorded in North Andhra and Rayalaseema districts. People are advised to take adequate precautions against intense sun and heat.
Meanwhile, Parvathipuram Manyam district recorded the highest temperature of 45°C followed by Kurnool (43.7°C), Nandyal (43.6°C), Anantapur (43.5°C), Vizianagaram, (43.4°C), YSR Kadapa (43.4°C) over the last 24 hours.
Yesterday’s trough, wind discontinuity from east Vidarbha to Comorin area across Telangana, Interior Karnataka and Tamilnadu at 0.9 km above mean sea level persists. Lower tropospheric southerly, south westerly winds prevail over AP and Yanam.
